config.seed_text: A forgotten machine beneath the sea continued speaking long after its makers were gone. Each repetition made its message stranger, denser, and more self-aware.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text:
As one would expect from such an experience when you get bored with something – for instance if your teacher or professor says "to" as well…) A human brain can not only process any stimulus within moments but also respond rapidly at will under natural conditions where humans may want to know how things got started (a common feature among those who use computer simulations). It has learned which rules apply throughout our lives so that we learn about ourselves based on what others told us first hand …

...but I'm getting ahead here again because this time all mental processes aren't going away either! If anything, they're likely just taking over before my eyes
